name: Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc.
business: Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a global biopharmaceutical company focused on the development and commercialization of innovative therapies for high unmet medical needs. Its commercial portfolio includes Fanapt (for bipolar I disorder and schizophrenia), HETLIOZ (for Non-24-Hour Sleep-Wake Disorder and Smith-Magenis syndrome), PONVORY (for relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis), and NEREUS (for prevention of vomiting induced by motion). The company also has a pipeline of products in development, including Bysanti, imsidolimab, and others.

exposures.sentence: Our commercial portfolio is currently comprised of four products: Fanapt for the acute treatment of manic or mixed episodes associated with bipolar I disorder and the treatment of schizophrenia, HETLIOZ for the treatment of Non-24-Hour Sleep-Wake Disorder (Non-24) and for the treatment of nighttime sleep disturbances in Smith-Magenis syndrome (SMS), PONVORY for the treatment of relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis (RMS) including clinically isolated syndrome, relapsing-remitting disease and active secondary progressive disease and NEREUS TM for the prevention of vomiting induced by motion (collectively, our commercial products).

In addition, we have a number of drugs and/or additional indications for current products in development, including: Fanapt (iloperidone) long acting injectable (LAI) formulation for the treatment of schizophrenia and hypertension; Bysanti TM (milsaperidone), the active metabolite of Fanapt , for the acute treatment of manic or mixed episodes associated with bipolar I disorder and for the treatment of schizophrenia and major depressive disorder (MDD); HETLIOZ (tasimelteon) for the treatment of jet lag disorder, insomnia, pediatric insomnia, delayed sleep phase disorder (DSPD) and pediatric Non-24; PONVORY (ponesimod) for the treatment of psoriasis and ulcerative colitis; NEREUS TM (tradipitant) for the prevention of vomiting induced by GLP-1 receptor agonists, the treatment of gastroparesis and the treatment of atopic dermatitis; Imsidolimab, an IL-36R antagonist, for the treatment of generalized pustular psoriasis (GPP); VTR-297, a small molecule histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor for the treatment of hematologic malignancies and onychomycosis and with potential use as a treatment for several oncology indications; Portfolio of Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator (CFTR) activators and inhibitors, including VSJ-110 for the treatment of dry eye and ocular inflammation and VPO-227 for the treatment of secretory diarrhea disorders, including cholera; VQW-765, a small molecule alpha-7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor partial agonist, for the treatment of social/performance anxiety and psychiatric disorders; and Antisense oligonucleotide (ASO) molecules, including VCA-894A for the treatment of Charcot-Marie-Tooth Disease, Type 2S (CMT2S), caused by cryptic slice site variants within the IGHMBP2 gene and VGT-1849A for the treatment of polycythemia vera (PV), a form of a rare hematologic malignancy.
In the U.S., HETLIOZ competes with generic versions of HETLIOZ and we could experience increased generic competition in the near term.

catalysts.event: PDUFA target action date for Bysanti NDA for bipolar I disorder and schizophrenia
catalysts.sentence: The FDA accepted our New Drug Application (NDA) for Bysanti TM for the treatment of bipolar I disorder and schizophrenia for filing in May 2025 and set a PDUFA target action date of February 21, 2026.

catalysts.event: Phase III clinical study results for Bysanti in MDD expected
catalysts.sentence: We initiated a Phase III clinical study for Bysanti TM as a once-daily adjunctive treatment for MDD in the fourth quarter of 2024, with results expected in 2026.

catalysts.event: FDA re-review of HETLIOZ sNDA for jet lag disorder concluded
catalysts.sentence: On January 8, 2026, we announced that we had received a decision letter from the FDA Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER) concluding that the supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) for HETLIOZ for the treatment of jet lag disorder cannot be approved in the current form.
In December 2025, we submitted the Biologics License Application (BLA) for imsidolimab for the treatment of GPP and requested priority review of the BLA.

catalysts.event: NEREUS Phase III program for GLP-1 induced vomiting anticipated to initiate
catalysts.sentence: A Phase III program is anticipated to initiate in the first half of 2026, positioning NEREUS TM as a key adjunct to improve outcomes in GLP-1 agonist treatments.

catalysts.event: VQW-765 Phase III study results expected
catalysts.sentence: A Phase III study of VQW-765 in the treatment of adults with social anxiety disorder has been initiated and study results are expected by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: Trial for HETLIOZ patents against Teva and Apotex scheduled to begin
catalysts.sentence: A trial is scheduled to begin in these cases on August 3, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Non-24 affects approximately 80,000 people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Non-24 affects a majority of totally blind individuals, or approximately 80,000 people in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: SMS is estimated to affect 1/15,000-25,000 births in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: SMS is estimated to affect 1/15,000-25,000 births in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 30% of the general population suffers from motion sickness.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 30% of the general population is reported to suffer from motion sickness under ordinary travel conditions that include sea, air and land travel.

tamClaims.claim: Gastroparesis prevalence estimated at approximately six million patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: A paper by Rey et al published in the January 2012 Journal of Neurogastroenterology and Motility estimated the prevalence of gastroparesis in the U.S. to be approximately six million patients, many of whom remain undiagnosed.

tamClaims.claim: Atopic dermatitis affects 9.8 million diagnosed individuals in the U.S., with 6.4 million drug-treated.
tamClaims.sentence: A 2015 Decision Resources Group report estimated that 9.8 million individuals were diagnosed with atopic dermatitis in the U.S., of which approximately 6.4 million were drug-treated atopic dermatitis patients.
The estimated prevalence of CMT is 1 in 2,500 individuals, with varying clinical features dependent on the various genetic variants of CMT.

tamClaims.claim: CMT2S prevalence is less than 1 in 1,000,000 worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: The prevalence of CMT2S is estimated to be less than 1 in 1,000,000 worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: DSPD affects approximately 1% of the population.
tamClaims.sentence: DSPD is likely the most prevalent circadian-rhythm sleep disorder, affecting approximately 1% of the population, and there is no FDA approved treatment at this time.

tamClaims.claim: More than 20 million U.S. residents make trips abroad each year to overseas destinations.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. Department of Commerce, International Trade Administration reports state that more than 20 million U.S. residents make trips abroad each year to overseas destinations in Europe, the Middle East and Asia.
